channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Scaling Point-in-Time Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.11889v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models trained on unrestricted internet corpora inevitably embed information from the future, introducing lookahead bias that compromises the validity of backtests and causal inference in finance and the social sciences. Point-in-time l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, fine-tuning, instruction, model, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CANDI: Contextual Alignment for Niche Domains Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2607.11891v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The deployment of large language models (LLMs) in specialized domains like medical diagnostics and financial advisory necessitates evaluating capabilities beyond general knowledge. Traditional question-answering benchmarks often fail to capture the nu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, retrieval, alignment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: G-SHARE: A Guideline-Based Structured Reasoning Framework for Human-Factor Event Diagnosis
item.description: arXiv:2607.11892v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human-factor event diagnosis is essential for learning from operational events in nuclear power plants, yet its quality depends strongly on expert interpretation of narrative reports and guideline-based reasoning.Existing data-driven or one-shot large...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, alignment, framework, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: I'm Sorry, but I Can't Help with Braille: Revealing Accessibility Failures in State-of-the-Art LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.11893v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) perform strongly on many language tasks, but their capability in structurally constrained, accessibility-critical modalities such as Braille remains unclear. We evaluate state-of-the-art LLMs on bidirectional Korean-Braill...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, instruction, fine-tuning, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TAKE: Trajectory-Aware Knowledge Estimation for Text Dataset Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2607.11898v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large-scale text corpora have become a quiet bottleneck in modern NLP, not just in storage, but in the accumulated cost of training, fine-tuning, and continual learning. We propose a text dataset distillation framework that reduces corpora to as littl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, compression, fine-tuning, framework, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Transforming LLMs into Efficient Cross-Encoders via Knowledge Distillation for RAG Reranking
item.description: arXiv:2607.11933v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-encoders achieve high reranking accuracy in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines but impose quadratic inference costs that limit real-time deployment. We address this by fine-tuning LLaMA 3 (8B) as a drop-in reranker using a two-stage ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, vector, LLM, instruction, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MAGE: Understanding Stability-Performance Trade-offs in Multi-component Prompt Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2607.11944v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How do different components of iterative prompt optimization interact, and what happens when they are combined? We investigate this through MAGE (Memory-Augmented Goal-directed Prompt Evolution), a controlled analysis framework for studying component ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, GPT, analysis, study, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Optimal Adaptive Market Making: A Theoretical Framework for High-Yield Liquidity Provision in Perpetual Futures Markets
item.description: arXiv:2607.11888v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We develop a rigorous theoretical framework for optimal market making in perpetual futures markets with zero maker fees. We model the market maker's problem as a stochastic optimal control problem on a filtered probability space, where the controls ar...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, API, framework, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: In-Context Reinforcement Learning under Non-Stationarity: A Survey
item.description: arXiv:2607.11906v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The development of decision-pretrained transformers, algorithm distillation, long-context meta-RL, and retrieval-augmented agents has renewed interest in in-context reinforcement learning (ICRL): the ability of a pretrained or fine-tuned decision mode...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, retrieval, in-context, context window, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Ontology-Amplified Distillation and Contextuality Auditing for Sovereign Enterprise Language Models: A Combined Proof-of-Mechanism and Negative-Results Method Study
item.description: arXiv:2607.11948v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Regulated financial institutions operating under data-residency rules need tenant-owned language models that can run inside the institution's perimeter. This paper combines two related FAOS studies into one mechanism-and-control article. First, it rep...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, fine-tuning, GPT, ICL, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GRID: Grammar-Railed Decoding for Enterprise SQL Generation
item.description: arXiv:2607.11951v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models can write SQL, but enterprise deployment demands more than plausible text: outputs must be syntactically valid, must respect per-role and per-schema policy, must carry provable (not best-effort) guarantees, must not slow down as ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, product, model, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Optimization Is Not All You Need
item.description: arXiv:2607.11977v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In 2019, OpenAI released two million GPT-2 outputs-ungrammatical, half broken-to aid the detection of machine-generated text. The alignment that produced their more fluent successors is usually regarded as an engineering achievement; we read it instea...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, GPT, alignment, model, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Designing Agent-Ready Websites for AI Web Agents: A Framework for Machine Readability, Actionability, and Decision Reliability
item.description: arXiv:2607.12056v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Online shopping is increasingly shifting toward a model in which AI agents independently search for products, compare options, evaluate constraints, and carry out parts of the purchasing process for users. Website design must now support both human an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, GPT, framework, paper, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Operationalising Multi-Dimensional Evaluation for Conversational Agents: A Scalable, Governed Pipeline with Selective Re-evaluation and Model Benchmarking
item.description: arXiv:2607.12085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating retail conversational agents requires methods beyond lexical-overlap metrics to assess intent alignment, factuality, helpfulness, clarity, tone, and overall response quality. Although LLM-as-a-judge methods provide scalable alternatives to ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, framework, alignment, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Semidirect Fourier Delta Attention: Phase-Controlled Delta Memory with Constructive Chunk-WY Kernels
item.description: arXiv:2607.11897v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Linear attention replaces softmax attention's growing KV cache with a fixed recurrent state, but this compression limits exact state tracking and long-context memory. We introduce \emph{Semidirect Fourier Delta Attention} (SFDA), a phase-controlled ge...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, attention, compression, product, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Query Visibility Changes KV-Cache Compression Rankings: A Matched-Budget Audit
item.description: arXiv:2607.11942v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: KV-cache compression methods are predominantly evaluated with the query appended to the context before compression -- a query-aware protocol. Yet the economic case for a compressed KV cache is reuse: compress a document once, answer many future questi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, attention, compression, model, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, context window, prompt, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, retrieval, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, retrieval, knowledge base, framework, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.
item.description: SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, retrieval, product, RAG, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Belief-reality separation lives in routing over a shared value slot in language models
item.description: arXiv:2607.11945v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Capable language models hold what a character believes apart from what is true: told "Anna believes the cup is blue; in reality it is red," they answer blue about Anna and red about the world. Where in the computation does that separation live? We sho...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, paper, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hybrid Continual Learning for Low-Resource Australian Aboriginal Language Identification
item.description: arXiv:2607.11946v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language identification is an important step toward integrating endangered Australian Aboriginal languages (AALs) into speech technologies supporting language revitalisation and digital inclusion. However, extreme data scarcity limits model performanc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, fine-tuning, experiment, model, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph Feedback Controls Consensus and Clique Formation in Open-Weight Language-Model Populations
item.description: arXiv:2607.12077v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent language-model systems increasingly route local interactions, yet the runtime interaction graph is often treated as an implementation detail. We study convention formation in open-weight LM populations spanning 1.1B-32B parameters with a n...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, memory, model, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Qubit-Efficient Quantum Search for Hyperdimensional Decomposition via Logarithmic Encoding
item.description: arXiv:2607.11936v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC) represents symbols using high-dimensional hypervectors of dimension $D$. In hypervector decomposition, the objective is to recover $F$ constituent hypervectors, each drawn from a codebook of size $N$, from a bound targ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, experiment, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CARE-LoRA: Compressed Activation REconstruction for Memory-Efficient LoRA
item.description: arXiv:2607.11940v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As the scale of large pre-trained models continues to grow, fine-tuning them under limited memory budgets has become increasingly challenging. Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), currently one of the most widely adopted parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, fine-tuning, ICL, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: OmniPMNet: Bridging discrete and gridded PM10 forecasts via omni-query neural processes
item.description: arXiv:2607.11896v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Forecasting particulate matter (PM10) requires both station-scale accuracy and continuous spatial fields, especially during severe dust storms. Chemical transport models (CTMs) provide gridded forecasts but retain local biases, whereas graph neural ne...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mirror Horizon: Viable Path Entropy as a Measure of Bounded Reflection
item.description: arXiv:2607.11937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mirror Theory proposes that an intelligent system should be studied not only by what it represents, but by what coherent continuations it can sustain under repeated reflection. We make this claim operational through \emph{viable path entropy} (VPE), a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, experiment, model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: code-review-graph - Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.
item.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - A practical Claude Code guide with clear mental models and copy-paste examples — setup, prompt engineering, slash commands, skills, hooks, subagents, agent teams, and MCP servers. Beginner path to power-user depth. Featured in Awesome Claude Code.

item.title: Graph-Based Detection of Disinformation Narrative Diffusion between Russian and Ukrainian Telegram Channels
item.description: arXiv:2607.11894v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Detecting disinformation narratives on social media is challenging due to the scale of amplification, rapid evolution, and linguistic variability of online content. We propose a graph-based framework for identifying and analyzing disinformation narrat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, API, framework, model, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Calibration-First Reward-Component Auditing for Reinforcement Learning Control in Smart Greenhouses
item.description: arXiv:2607.11959v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Greenhouse reinforcement learning can test climate-control ideas at a speed and scale that is difficult to achieve with crop experiments alone. For smart-greenhouse control, however, a single simulator return is not enough: a grower or control enginee...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LP Mining with LP2Graph: A Use Case for Railway Rescheduling
item.description: arXiv:2607.11980v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Like many optimization-driven domains, railway rescheduling relies on Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP), yet the field's modeling knowledge is scattered across hundreds of papers in incompatible notations, and narrative surveys organize it subje...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Repairing Shape-Prior Shortcuts in Long-Range Single-Shot Fringe Projection Profilometry
item.description: arXiv:2607.11928v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Single-shot fringe projection profilometry (FPP) networks that regress depth directly can exploit a shape-prior shortcut, recovering depth from object boundaries rather than from fringe phase. On a photorealistic synthetic benchmark (15,600 fringe ima...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, arxiv, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BattVAE-GP: Generative Modeling of Long-Horizon Battery Degradation with Uncertainty Quantification
item.description: arXiv:2607.11943v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-horizon physics-based simulations of battery degradation provide mechanistic insight but remain computationally expensive, limiting their use for dense exploration of operating conditions over extended cycle life. Here, we propose a hybrid physic...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Generalized Distribution-Free Semi-Supervised Learning with Risk Rewrite
item.description: arXiv:2607.11947v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Typical semi-supervised learning (SSL) methods rely on distributional assumptions, and their performance degrades when these are violated. While PNU learning, a risk rewriting method, offers a distribution-free alternative, it is restricted to binary ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, arxiv, paper, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: WrenAI - GenBI (Generative BI) for AI agents, an open-source, governed text-to-SQL through an open context layer that turns natural-language questions into trusted dashboards, charts, and SQL across 20+ data sources, such as BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Amazon Redshift, Databricks and more.
item.description: GenBI (Generative BI) for AI agents, an open-source, governed text-to-SQL through an open context layer that turns natural-language questions into trusted dashboards, charts, and SQL across 20+ data sources, such as BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Amazon Redshift, Databricks and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Model Routing Is Simple. Until It Isn’t.
